body.title: [sprint] Convert inference callers to request tiers (Igor/IntentExtractor/Evaluator/audits)
body.intention: I intend that inference callers request a capability tier, not a specific model, so routing survives model churn — and that finishing this restores Igor's voice, proven.
body.description:
**Problem:** callers pass specific model IDs (anthropic/claude-haiku-4-5, llama3, gpt-4o-mini) which are fragile as models evolve and which trip the dead fallthrough. They should request a TIER. This ticket is the DOWN-PAYMENT on the KNOWN callers; T-inference-specific-model-alarm's caller-breakdown is the authoritative punch-list for the rest.

**Shape:** Convert IntentExtractorDevice, Igor cognition (map its on_tier/tier.* to inference task_class), EvaluatorDevice, and the audit-skill inference calls to request a task_class/tier instead of a model id. Absorbs T-intent-extractor-graceful-degradation: IntentExtractor stops silently dropping intention when inference is off — it surfaces via the system_alarms / honest-degradation path instead.

**Affected files:** devices/igor/cognition/* (on_tier->task_class mapping), IntentExtractorDevice, EvaluatorDevice, audit-skill inference calls.
**Design rules:** tiers-not-models; honest degradation (surface, don't silently drop).
**Scope boundary:** IN — convert the known callers + Igor tier mapping. OUT — the routing engine (T-inference-resolve-requests-by-tier); the alarm (T-inference-specific-model-alarm) which finds stragglers.
**Test plan:** grep shows the converted callers pass a tier/task_class, not a specific model id; a LIVE Igor chat round-trip returns text (end-to-end restore — depends on T-inference-resolve-requests-by-tier + T-inference-empty-response-fallthrough having shipped); IntentExtractor on inference-down surfaces an explicit unavailable signal, not a silent drop.
**Completion criteria:** converted callers request tiers (grep: no specific model ids in their dispatch); a live Igor round-trip returns a response (Igor talks again — proof); IntentExtractor no longer silently drops on inference-down. Absorbs T-intent-extractor-graceful-degradation.

[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)

[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)

[error] datacenter_logs/<device>/<subsystem>/ is the log hierarchy. Never write to a flat — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] PATTERN-001: BaseDevice / BaseShim Lifecycle: `rollback()` must be idempotent — safe to call even if — applies: tags: pattern, pattern-001 (source: docs/design_patterns_inventory.md#PATTERN-001: BaseDevice / BaseShim Lifecycle)
